name: Tuna Stew

ingredients.item: canned peeled roma tomatoes, broken up with your fingers
freshly shelled peas sea salt and ground black pepper

method: Heat the butter and oil in a large saucepan and sauté the onion until softened, then add the garlic, being careful not to let it brown. Add the carrots, celery and herbs and cook for a further few minutes.

Add the tomato paste and anchovies and cook over medium heat for about 5 minutes. Add the peeled tomatoes and turn the heat down to low. Put a lid on the pan and cook for 30 minutes, stirring from time to time.
Put the tuna in the pan, breaking it into bite-sized pieces with the spoon, and then add the peas. Cook until the peas are tender (about 10 minutes).
Taste the stew for salt and pepper, discard the bay leaf, and serve.
This wintry dish is traditionally served with polenta, for soaking up the delicious juices (see Polenta Pasticciata for a basic polenta recipe). It is also great served with mashed potatoes.
